senses.definition: a type of gymnastics involving graceful movements with apparatus such as ribbons, hoops, and balls

examples.x: She has been practicing rhythmic gymnastics since she was six years old.
The rhythmic gymnastics team won gold at the regional competition.
Elena's favorite apparatus in rhythmic gymnastics is the ribbon.
Rhythmic gymnastics combines elements of ballet, gymnastics, and dance.
They performed a breathtaking routine in rhythmic gymnastics at the Olympics.

senses.definition: a discipline of gymnastics that involves routines on apparatuses emphasizing strength, flexibility, and artistry

examples.x: Artistic gymnastics demands precision and flexibility.
The vault is a thrilling event in artistic gymnastics.
Judges assess difficulty and execution in artistic gymnastics.
He clinched gold in artistic gymnastics at nationals.
Artistic gymnastics includes floor exercise and uneven bars.

senses.definition: a gymnastics sport involving acrobatic movements performed on a trampoline

examples.x: Olympic trampolining athletes undergo rigorous training schedules.
She excels in trampolining, executing flips and twists effortlessly.

senses.definition: special moves such as walking on a tight rope, swinging in the air using certain equipment, etc.

examples.x: Acrobatics showcases impressive physical feats such as balancing, tumbling, and aerial maneuvers.
She trained for years to perfect her acrobatics skills, performing daring stunts on the trapeze.
The circus performers amazed the audience with their acrobatics routine, including flips and somersaults.
Acrobatics requires strength, flexibility, and coordination to execute complex movements.
Gymnastics incorporates acrobatics as athletes perform floor routines and balance beam exercises.

senses.definition: a gymnastics position where the legs are extended in opposite directions, either sideways or front and back

examples.x: She performed a flawless split during her floor exercise routine.
Achieving a full split requires consistent training and flexibility exercises.
He stretched daily to maintain his ability to execute a split.
Flexibility drills are essential for gymnasts aiming to perfect their splits.

senses.definition: a body position in gymnastics where the legs are straight and held together, while the upper body is bent forward at the waist

examples.x: She executed a perfect pike during her floor routine.
Keep your back straight when transitioning into a pike.
Beginners often struggle to maintain a tight pike.
Practice your pike to enhance your overall routine.

senses.definition: a movement in gymnastics or dance where a one turns on one foot with the other leg lifted to the knee

examples.x: Her pique turn sequence was a highlight of the performance.
During practice, she focused on perfecting her pique turn technique.
She executed a flawless pique turn during her gymnastics routine.
The choreography called for a series of pique turns across the stage.
The dance troupe synchronized their pique turns perfectly.

senses.definition: a gymnastics position where the legs are spread wide apart while seated or standing, often with the torso upright

examples.x: She executed a perfect straddle on the balance beam.
She stretched her legs into a wide straddle for her routine on the parallel bars.
Spectators applauded as he held a straddle on the rings with ease.
Gymnasts practice their straddle positions to improve flexibility and form.
Flexibility exercises help gymnasts achieve a deeper straddle stretch.

senses.definition: a gymnastics position where the body is inverted and supported upright by the hands, with arms straight and shoulders aligned over the wrists

examples.x: She held a perfect handstand for several seconds during her routine.
Gymnasts practice handstands to improve core strength and balance.
The gymnast demonstrated impeccable form in her handstand on the beam.
Coaches teach proper hand placement and body alignment for a stable handstand.
He performed a one-arm handstand as part of his floor routine.

senses.definition: a gymnastic competition refers to an event where participants compete in multiple categories to determine an overall winner
(gymnastics) a routine performed on a padded floor mat, showcasing tumbling, dance, and acrobatic skills

examples.x: She excelled in the floor exercise, earning a perfect score with her graceful routine.
The gymnast practiced her floor exercise tirelessly to perfect her routine for the competition.
His favorite part of gymnastics was always the floor exercise because of the freedom it allowed.
The gymnast displayed incredible flexibility and strength in her floor exercise.
His passion for the sport was evident in every leap and jump of his floor exercise.

senses.definition: the governing body for gymnastics worldwide

examples.x: The International Gymnastics Federation sets the rules and regulations for all gymnastics competitions.
To qualify for the Olympics, gymnasts must meet the criteria established by the International Gymnastics Federation.

senses.definition: to perform a gymnastic or acrobatic movement in which the body makes a complete revolution, typically forwards or backwards, with the feet passing over the head

examples.x: The gymnast somersaulted gracefully across the mat, showcasing her agility and skill.
The acrobat somersaulted through the air, executing a series of flips and twists in the breathtaking routine.
In the thrilling finale, the circus performer somersaulted through a ring of fire, leaving the crowd in awe.
Filled with enthusiasm, the kids somersaulted and tumbled in the gymnastics class.
The trapeze artist elegantly somersaults from one bar to another, captivating the audience below.

senses.definition: to perform a gymnastic move involving rolling the body sideways in a full circle, typically with arms and legs extended

examples.x: The gymnast effortlessly cartwheeled across the mat, showcasing her agility and grace.
Inspired by the circus performer, the child attempted to cartwheel in the backyard.
After scoring the winning goal, the soccer player couldn't contain his joy and cartwheeled in celebration.
Delighted by the news, he cartwheeled out of the office, thrilled about the unexpected promotion.
The playful puppy cartwheeled in the backyard, reveling in the freedom of the open space.

senses.definition: to leap or spring over an obstacle with the aid of hands or a pole

examples.x: Last year, she confidently vaulted over the stream during the track and field competition.
During the dance routine, the performer gracefully vaulted across the stage, captivating the audience.
With a running start, the pole vaulter expertly vaulted over the bar, setting a new record.
In the parkour routine, the traceur confidently vaulted over walls and railings with fluidity.
